svg.style: font-family:Lato, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, 'Liberation Sans', sans-serif;font-feature-settings:"liga", "kern", "calt", "lnum";text-rendering:geometricPrecision;-webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased;font-size:18px;background-color:#ffffff

a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Daily vs. total confirmed COVID-19 cases per million people
Shown is the 7-day rolling average of confirmed COVID-19 cases per million people. The number of confirmed
cases is lower than the number of total cases. The main reason for this is limited testing.

Total confirmed cases per million
(cases per million people; plotted on a logarithmic axis)

Daily confirmed cases per million
(cases per million people; plotted on a logarithmic axis)
Data source: World Health Organization (2026); Population based on various sources (2024)
